tasks.title: Initialize Repository & Toolchain
tasks.description: Create monorepo with backend (Python/FastAPI) and frontend (React/Vite) workspaces. Configure package managers, lint/format, pre-commit hooks, CI skeleton.
tasks.details:
• git init, set main branch
• pnpm / pipenv with lock files
• Directory layout:
  /backend  (FastAPI, uvicorn)
  /frontend (React + TS)
  /infra    (Docker, terraform stubs)
• Dockerfile & docker-compose with SQLite volume mount
• CI: GitHub Actions – lint, unit-test, build images
• README with local dev commands
tasks.testStrategy: Run `docker compose up`, hit health endpoint http://localhost:8000/health returns 200. CI passes lint & sample test.

subtasks.title: Initialize Git Repository & Monorepo Root
subtasks.description: Create the base Git repository, root configuration and initial commit for the monorepo.
subtasks.details:
• git init and create main branch
• Add .gitignore covering Python, Node, VSCode, macOS and Docker artifacts
• Create root README.md with project overview and contributor guidelines placeholder
• Establish directory skeleton: /backend, /frontend, /infra, /data, /.github
• Add LICENSE (MIT unless specified otherwise)
• Commit all above as “chore(repo): initial commit”
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `git status` shows clean working tree. Verify directories exist and README contains high-level sections.

subtasks.title: Bootstrap Backend Workspace (Python/FastAPI)
subtasks.description: Scaffold backend service, dependency manager and code-quality tooling.
subtasks.details:
• Inside /backend run `pipenv --python 3.11` then install FastAPI, uvicorn[standard]
• Generate Pipfile.lock with `pipenv lock`
• Scaffold FastAPI app: main.py exposing GET /health returning {"status":"ok"}
• Add tests/ with pytest sample checking /health returns 200
• Configure lint/format:
  – black, isort, flake8 in dev dependencies
  – pyproject.toml with shared config
• Add pre-commit config entries for black, isort, flake8 (to be activated later)
• Commit as “feat(backend): scaffold FastAPI service”
subtasks.testStrategy: In /backend run `pipenv run pytest` succeeds. Running `pipenv run uvicorn main:app --reload` then hitting /health returns 200 JSON.

subtasks.title: Bootstrap Frontend Workspace (React/Vite + pnpm)
subtasks.description: Create React + TypeScript frontend, package manager config and JS/TS code-quality tooling.
subtasks.details:
• At repo root run `pnpm init` to create root package.json with `workspaces:["frontend"]`
• Inside /frontend run `pnpm create vite@latest` (react-ts template)
• Configure eslint, prettier and @typescript-eslint; add scripts `lint` and `format`
• Generate .eslintrc.cjs and .prettierrc with agreed rules
• Add sample component calling /api/health and printing response
• Commit as “feat(frontend): scaffold React Vite workspace”
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm --filter frontend dev` — app loads at http://localhost:5173 and health call succeeds when backend running. Execute `pnpm lint` shows zero errors.

subtasks.title: Add Containerization & Orchestration
subtasks.description: Provide Dockerfiles for each service and a docker-compose stack for local development, including persistent SQLite volume.
subtasks.details:
• /backend/Dockerfile: python:3.11-slim, copy source, install via pipenv, expose 8000, CMD uvicorn
• /frontend/Dockerfile: node:20-alpine, build Vite app, serve with nginx or vite preview
• docker-compose.yml at root:
  – backend service building /backend image, maps 8000:8000
  – frontend service building /frontend image, maps 3000:80
  – bind-mount ./data to /data inside backend container for united_airlines_normalized.db
• Define named volume sqlite_data for persistence
• Add Makefile targets `make dev` (compose up) & `make clean`
• Commit as “chore(dev-env): docker & compose setup”
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `docker compose up -d`, wait for containers, curl http://localhost:8000/health returns 200. Frontend served at http://localhost:3000 shows page.

subtasks.title: Configure Developer Workflow: Pre-Commit & CI Skeleton
subtasks.description: Wire up pre-commit hooks and GitHub Actions pipelines for linting, tests and image builds, plus finalize README.
subtasks.details:
• Activate pre-commit: `pre-commit install` at root linking to config that runs black, isort, flake8, eslint, prettier
• /.github/workflows/ci.yml with jobs:
  – setup-python & run backend lint+tests
  – setup-node & run frontend lint+unit tests
  – build backend/frontend Docker images and push to GHCR (if GH_PAT available)
• Add status badge to README and document local dev commands (`pipenv shell`, `pnpm dev`, `docker compose up`)
• Commit as “ci: add GitHub Actions & pre-commit hooks”
subtasks.testStrategy: Push branch to GitHub; workflow passes all jobs. Run `pre-commit run --all-files` locally to ensure zero failures. README displays correct commands when rendered.

tasks.title: Integrate united_airlines_normalized.db
tasks.description: Add read-only data access layer to SQLite database used for answering queries.
tasks.details:
• Place DB inside /data; set env var DB_PATH
• SQLAlchemy models or simple raw SQL views for: flights, equipment, personnel, shifts, breaks
• Create DAL class with memoized prepared statements for sample queries
• Index critical columns (flight_no, gate, employee_id) for <10ms lookup
• Provide DAO methods: get_flight_status, get_equipment_by_flight, get_employee_phone, etc.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with pytest & SQLite in-memory copy. Assert queries return expected rows matching PRD examples.

subtasks.title: Add database file and configuration
subtasks.description: Copy united_airlines_normalized.db into the repository, mount it at /data in Docker, and expose its location through the DB_PATH environment variable for both local and containerised execution.
subtasks.details:
• Create /data directory at repo root and place united_airlines_normalized.db inside it.
• Update .gitignore so the DB is not ignored.
• In docker-compose.yml and backend Dockerfile, add a volume mapping ./data:/app/data and ENV DB_PATH=/app/data/united_airlines_normalized.db.
• For local runs, add `export DB_PATH="$(pwd)/data/united_airlines_normalized.db"` to backend/.env or dev script.
• Verify file accessibility with `python -c 'import os,sqlite3,sys; sqlite3.connect(os.getenv("DB_PATH")); print("ok")'`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `docker compose up -d backend` then exec into the container and execute `sqlite3 $DB_PATH '.tables'`; expect tables flights, equipment, personnel, shifts, breaks to be listed.

subtasks.title: Define read-only schema models or SQL views
subtasks.description: Expose logical tables (flights, equipment, personnel, shifts, breaks) via SQLAlchemy declarative models or, if preferred, create immutable SQL views referencing the existing tables.
subtasks.details:
• In backend/db/models.py create SQLAlchemy Base.
• Use reflection (automap) or explicit model definitions matching existing columns and primary keys.
• Mark all models with __tablename__, primary_key, and nullable flags; set __table_args__ = {'sqlite_autoincrement': False}.
• Alternatively, execute CREATE VIEW statements if the physical tables need renaming/aliasing.
• Ensure models are read-only by not exposing session add/commit; only `Session(bind=engine, autoflush=False, autocommit=False)` for SELECT.
subtasks.testStrategy: Instantiate a session and query each model: `session.query(Flights).limit(1).all()` etc. Assert no exceptions and objects have expected attribute names.

subtasks.title: Implement Data Access Layer with memoized prepared statements
subtasks.description: Create a DAL/DAO class that opens a single SQLAlchemy engine against DB_PATH and exposes high-level read methods (get_flight_status, get_equipment_by_flight, get_employee_phone, …). Prepared statements should be compiled once and cached.
subtasks.details:
• backend/db/dal.py: define class `AirlineDAL`.
• On `__init__`, build `self.engine = create_engine(f'sqlite:///{DB_PATH}', connect_args={'check_same_thread': False})` and `self.sessionmaker = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)`.
• Use `functools.lru_cache(maxsize=None)` or a class-level dict to cache `text()` compiled statements, e.g. `SELECT status FROM flights WHERE flight_no = :flight_no LIMIT 1`.
• Methods should open a session context (`with self.sessionmaker() as s:`) and execute cached statements with parameters, returning domain objects or dicts.
• Add graceful error handling for missing rows (return None).
subtasks.testStrategy: Call each DAO method with a known key from seed data; assert returned value matches direct SQL query result.

subtasks.title: Create performance indexes on critical columns
subtasks.description: Add SQLite indexes for flight_no, gate, and employee_id to guarantee sub-10 ms lookups for typical query sizes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run pytest benchmark that executes get_flight_status 1 000 times; assert mean duration <0.01 s and that `PRAGMA index_info` shows expected columns.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests and CI integration for DAL
subtasks.description: Cover all DAO methods with pytest using an in-memory copy of the production DB and integrate the tests into the existing GitHub Actions workflow.
subtasks.details:
• Add backend/tests/test_dal.py.
• In tests, create a temporary SQLite :memory: connection and `conn.executescript(open(DB_PATH).read())` to copy schema & data.
• Monkeypatch AirlineDAL.DB_PATH to point to the :memory: database.
• Parametrize tests for each DAO method with sample inputs/expected outputs derived from fixture rows.
• Add performance test (pytest-benchmark) ensuring ≤10 ms average query time.
• Update .github/workflows/backend.yml to run `pytest -q`.
subtasks.testStrategy: CI run must pass all dal tests with >95% coverage; deliberately break a DAO query locally to confirm tests fail.

tasks.title: Natural Language Query Parser & Answer Generator
tasks.description: Translate user utterances into structured DB queries and craft spoken answers with confidence scores.
tasks.details:
• Use spaCy + rule-based regex + few-shot OpenAI function calling (NO internet knowledge retrieval) but pass schema so answers only from DB
• Pipeline: intent detection ➜ slot extraction ➜ DAO call ➜ structured result ➜ answer template
• Confidence: if intent score <0.75 or DAO returns none ⇒ fallback
• Pseudocode:
```python
result,intent_score=parse(utterance)
if intent_score<0.75 or not result:
    return Answer(text="I don't know", confidence=intent_score)
```
• Unit templates for each Q type stored as Jinja2.
tasks.testStrategy: Parametrized tests for provided test questions – expect exact answers. Add fuzzed paraphrases checking ≥0.9 bleu match.

subtasks.title: Implement Intent Detection Module
subtasks.description: Create a component that classifies user utterances into predefined intent labels and returns a confidence score.
subtasks.details:
• Build a spaCy pipeline with custom text-categorizer component trained on annotated intents.
• Add rule-based regex matchers for high-precision patterns (e.g., ^how many, ^list all …).
• Wrap few-shot OpenAI function-calling fallback: pass utterance + intent schema, parse model response to {intent,score}.
• Combine scores via weighted average; expose function detect_intent(text)->(intent,score).
subtasks.testStrategy:
Unit: parametrized pytest with 50 labeled utterances, assert predicted intent == gold and score>=0.75 for ≥90% of cases.
Stress: fuzz 200 paraphrases via NL paraphraser, ensure no crash and score is float between 0–1.

subtasks.title: Develop Slot Extraction & Entity Parsing
subtasks.description: Extract required entities/slots from the utterance based on the detected intent.
subtasks.details:
• For each intent, define slot schema (e.g., {product_name,date_range}).
• Use spaCy NER models + custom EntityRuler patterns; attach regex post-processing for dates, numbers.
• If slots missing or ambiguous, call OpenAI function that returns structured slot dict restricted to schema.
• Output parse_slots(text,intent)->(slots:dict,missing:list).
subtasks.testStrategy:
Unit: provide 30 utterances per intent with expected slot dict; assert exact match.
Edge: missing slot input should return in missing list.

subtasks.title: Build DAO Query Builder & Executor
subtasks.description: Convert intent and extracted slots into parameterized SQL queries and fetch results from the database.
subtasks.details:
• Map each intent to SQL template with named params.
• Implement QueryBuilder that fills template with slot values, guarding against SQL injection via parameter binding.
• Create DAO layer using async SQLAlchemy; execute query, return result as list[dict].
• Handle empty result with None.
subtasks.testStrategy:
Integration: spin up Docker Postgres with seed data; for each intent/slot pair run builder+DAO, assert returned rows match fixtures.
Security: attempt SQL injection in slots, verify parameter binding prevents it.

subtasks.title: Design Answer Template Renderer
subtasks.description: Generate natural-language answers from structured DAO results using Jinja2 templates.
subtasks.details:
• For each intent, create Jinja2 template files (e.g., templates/answers/<intent>.j2) with placeholders for result fields.
• Implement render_answer(intent,result)->text that loads template, supplies context, and falls back to generic ‘no data’ template if result None.
• Support pluralization & numeric formatting helpers.
subtasks.testStrategy:
Unit: snapshot test rendering for sample results; compare against stored golden strings.
Pluralization test: singular vs plural counts produce grammatically correct output.

subtasks.title: Integrate End-to-End Parser & Answer Generator with Confidence Logic
subtasks.description: Orchestrate intent detection, slot extraction, DAO call, and template rendering into a single pipeline that returns Answer objects with confidence handling.
subtasks.details:
• Implement parse_and_answer(utterance)->Answer dataclass {text,confidence}.
• Steps: (1) intent,score=detect_intent; (2) slots=extract_slots; (3) result=query_DAO; (4) text=render_answer.
• Apply fallback rules: if score<0.75 or result is None -> text="I don't know".
• Expose REST endpoint POST /api/nlq {utterance} returning JSON Answer.
• Add logging of intermediate artifacts for metrics task.
subtasks.testStrategy:
End-to-end tests: feed 20 utterances, assert HTTP 200 and expected text.
Fallback test: force low intent score & empty result, expect "I don't know" with propagated confidence.
Performance: ensure avg latency <500 ms excluding OpenAI calls.

tasks.title: Wake-Word Detection Service
tasks.description: Always-on wake word listener for "Jarvis" / "Hey Jarvis" with <100ms detection.
tasks.details:
• Integrate Picovoice Porcupine WASM in browser for on-device detection (no network latency)
• Expose Boolean state to React context; start streaming audio only after wake detected
• Sensitivity 0.6, keyword model JARVIS.ppn
tasks.testStrategy: Manual: speak sentences containing and not containing wake word 50 times; expect ≥95% correct. Automated: play pre-recorded wav files in jest test with Porcupine node binding.

subtasks.title: Add Picovoice Porcupine WASM assets and loader
subtasks.description: Install Porcupine JS SDK, place JARVIS.ppn model in public assets, and create an async loader that initializes the Porcupine WASM engine with the specified sensitivity (0.6).
subtasks.details:
• Add `@picovoice/porcupine-web-en-worker` (or latest) to /frontend via pnpm.
• Copy `JARVIS.ppn` into `/frontend/public/models`.
• Implement `porcupineLoader.ts` that:
   – Dynamically imports the WASM worker to avoid blocking main thread.
   – Accepts config `{keywordPath, sensitivity}` and resolves an initialized engine instance.
   – Handles graceful failure (e.g., unsupported browser) by throwing descriptive errors.
• Export singleton promise so engine is created once.
• Verify engine loads in <50 ms on modern laptop with `performance.now()`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit: mock fetch of WASM + model, assert loader resolves and exposes `process()` method. Measure initialization time <150 ms.

subtasks.title: Implement Microphone capture and Porcupine processing loop
subtasks.description: Stream microphone PCM frames to Porcupine in a Web Worker, emitting a wake-detected event within 100 ms after spoken phrase.
subtasks.details:
• Request `getUserMedia({audio: true, video: false})` with echoCancellation disabled.
• Down-sample to Porcupine’s required sample rate (e.g., 16 kHz) using `AudioWorklet`.
• In a dedicated worker, call `porcupineWorker.process(frame)` for each 512-sample frame.
• Post a `{"type":"wake", "timestamp": Date.now()}` message when result==true.
• Expose an API `startListening()` / `stopListening()` that manages tracks and worker lifecycle.
• Benchmark end-to-end detection latency with `console.time` around phrase playback; optimize by reducing buffer size until <100 ms.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration: play pre-recorded wav containing the wake word through `AudioContext` and assert event received <100 ms after phrase end.

subtasks.title: Create React Context for wake state
subtasks.description: Provide a `WakeWordProvider` that exposes `{isAwake, reset}` through `useWakeWord()` hook, updating state upon worker events.
subtasks.details:
• Initialize provider on mount by calling `startListening()` from subtask 4.2.
• Maintain internal `const [isAwake, setIsAwake] = useState(false)`.
• On received `wake` event, set `isAwake=true` and start a 10-second timer to auto-reset.
• Expose `reset()` to manually clear state.
• Memoize context value with `useMemo` to avoid re-renders.
• Add SSR guard (check `typeof window !== 'undefined'`).
subtasks.testStrategy: React Testing Library: render provider, simulate postMessage, expect `isAwake` true, call `reset()` and expect false.

subtasks.title: Gate downstream audio streaming with wake state
subtasks.description: Modify existing audio streaming module so that it only begins transmitting microphone data to backend after `isAwake` becomes true and stops when reset.
subtasks.details:
• In `useAudioStreamer()` hook, consume `useWakeWord()`.
• When `isAwake` flips true, call `startStreaming()` (already implemented in task 3); when false, call `stopStreaming()`.
• Ensure no audio frames leave the browser before wake detection to preserve privacy and bandwidth.
• Add exponential back-off reconnect logic so dropped streams restart only if still awake.
• Log state transitions with `debug` library for observability.
subtasks.testStrategy: End-to-end browser test (Playwright): speak sentence with wake word → stream WebSocket frames count >0; speak without wake word → frames ==0.

subtasks.title: Comprehensive testing & tuning for accuracy and latency
subtasks.description: Automate and document validation: ≥95 % accuracy and <100 ms latency using prerecorded samples plus manual runs.
subtasks.details:
• Place 50 positive and 50 negative wav clips in `/tests/audio`.
• Use `@picovoice/porcupine-node` in Jest to batch-process files, asserting true positives ≥95 % and false positives ≤2 %.
• Add Playwright script to load app, play clips via virtual mic (Loopback on Mac/VoiceMeeter on Windows), and log detection time with `performance.now()`.
• Plot confusion matrix and latency histogram, save to `/reports/wake_word.html`.
• Adjust sensitivity (0.5-0.7 sweep) if thresholds not met; commit final chosen value in `.env`.
• Document setup and results in `docs/wake_word_testing.md`.
subtasks.testStrategy: CI step `pnpm test:wake` runs node + Playwright suites; pipeline fails if accuracy or latency thresholds not met.

tasks.title: Speech-to-Text Capture
tasks.description: Convert microphone input to text after wake. Aim <800ms recognition latency.
tasks.details:
• Use Web Speech API (Chrome) fallback to Deepgram streaming REST
• Chunk size 100ms; accumulate until 500ms silence
• Send final transcript to backend `/api/query`
tasks.testStrategy: Cypress test: speak canned audio, assert transcript equals expected string (within Levenshtein ≤2). Measure end-to-end time.

subtasks.title: Microphone Stream & 100 ms Audio Buffer
subtasks.description: After the wake-word callback fires, request microphone permission, open an AudioWorklet (fallback ScriptProcessor), down-sample to 16 kHz mono, and push raw PCM frames into a ring buffer emitting 100 ms Uint8Array chunks.
subtasks.details:
• Call navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({audio:true}) and immediately resume the AudioContext to avoid autoplay restrictions.
• Inside the worklet’s process(), accumulate samples until sampleCount ≥ 1600 (≈100 ms @16 kHz), then postMessage the chunk to main thread.
• Expose start(), stop(), and abort() controls returning an AsyncIterable so downstream recognisers can `for-await` audio chunks.
• Include back-pressure (pause reading) when no consumer is attached.
subtasks.testStrategy: Jest + jest-mock-getusermedia: feed a prerecorded WAV, assert emitted chunk count ≈ recordingLength/100 ms and chunks are 1600 samples long.

subtasks.title: Primary STT Wrapper using Web Speech API
subtasks.description: Create WebSpeechRecognizer class that subscribes to the chunk stream from 5.1, pipes audio into SpeechRecognition, and exposes ‘partial’ and ‘final’ transcript events with timestamps.
subtasks.details:
• Feature-detect window.SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition; instantiate with interimResults=true, continuous=true.
• On every `result` event, emit {text, isFinal, startTime, eventTime} to a central EventEmitter.
• Restart recognition automatically on `end` as long as microphone stream remains active.
• Measure latency: `eventTime - startTime` and attach to emitted object.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test with a stubbed SpeechRecognition implementation returning scripted interim/final results; assert class re-emits the same text and captures latency values.

subtasks.title: Deepgram Streaming Fallback Layer
subtasks.description: Implement DeepgramStreamingRecognizer that activates when 5.2 throws or browser lacks SpeechRecognition, sending 100 ms chunks over a websocket to Deepgram and converting JSON responses into the same event schema.
subtasks.details:
• Open wss://api.deepgram.com/v1/listen?punctuate=true&model=general using fetch API token from env.
• Marshal each Uint8Array chunk from 5.1 as linear16 PCM and send; await `message` events, parse `channel.alternatives[0]`.
• Emit partial/final events identical to 5.2 so upper layers stay agnostic.
• Auto-reconnect with exponential back-off ≤3 attempts.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test against Deepgram sandbox key with 3-second canned WAV; assert final transcript equality (Levenshtein ≤2) and no uncaught promise rejections.

subtasks.title: Silence Detection & Transcript Finalizer (500 ms)
subtasks.description: Consume transcript events from whichever recognizer is active (5.2 or 5.3) plus raw audio energy from 5.1, detect ≥500 ms of silence to mark utterance end, and guarantee final transcript emission within 800 ms.
subtasks.details:
• Compute RMS energy per 100 ms chunk; maintain sliding 5-chunk window. If all below threshold (configurable, start at ‑50 dB FS), signal ‘silence’.
• On silence AND last transcript `.isFinal==true`, close current recognizer stream, flush buffers, and resolve a Promise<Transcript> with timing info (speechStart → finalEvent).
• Use `performance.now()` to enforce a hard timeout: if recognizer hasn’t produced final text within 800 ms after silence, forcibly take the latest interim and mark `lowConfidence=true`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Simulated audio containing speech-silence-speech pattern; expect finalizer to split into correct utterances and latency histogram median <800 ms.

subtasks.title: Backend Dispatch & Latency Metrics
subtasks.description: Post finalized transcript to /api/query, include latency fields, and expose an event for UI listeners; retry network failures with exponential back-off (≤3 s total).
subtasks.details:
• Build payload: {text, speechStart, finalTime, recognitionLatency}.
• `fetch('/api/query', {method:'POST', body:JSON.stringify(payload)})` with AbortController 2 s timeout.
• Fire custom DOM event ‘stt:final’ carrying payload so Task 7 UI can add transcript bubble.
• Record metrics via window.performance.mark/measure and push to window.STTMetrics array for Task 8 collection.
subtasks.testStrategy: Cypress: mock /api/query with cy.intercept, speak sample audio, assert POST body matches expected transcript and that `recognitionLatency` ≤800 ms.

tasks.title: Backend Text-to-Speech (TTS) Endpoint
tasks.testStrategy: Unit: send sample and verify 200 + audio bytes length>0. Subjective listening of 5 sentences.

subtasks.title: Select & Configure TTS Provider
subtasks.description: Decide between AWS Polly (cloud) and Coqui TTS (offline) and set up corresponding environment so that the rest of the code can call a unified interface.
subtasks.details:
• Add `TTS_PROVIDER` env var with values `polly` or `coqui`.
• For AWS Polly: install `boto3`, create IAM user with `polly:SynthesizeSpeech`, store access keys via `.env` or AWS default chain.
• For Coqui: add `TTS_MODELS_DIR` env var; use `tts` Python package; download a lightweight English model during image build (or at first run) into this dir.
• Write thin provider classes `PollyProvider` and `CoquiProvider` each exposing `synthesize(text:str, voice:str, fmt:str)->bytes`.
• Implement factory `get_tts_provider()` that returns the selected provider instance based on env.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run provider self-tests: call `synthesize('hello', 'default', 'mp3')` and assert byte length > 0 for both providers (mock Polly with `botocore.stub.Stubber`).

subtasks.title: Implement Cached TTS Service Module
subtasks.description: Create a service layer that hashes text, checks Redis cache, invokes provider, and stores result with appropriate metadata and TTL.
subtasks.details:
• Install `redis-py` client; expect `REDIS_URL` env var.
• Define `hash_text(text:str)->str` using SHA256 and maybe voice+fmt salt.
• Function `get_or_generate_audio(text, voice='Joanna', fmt='mp3')`:
    1. `key = f"tts:{hash_text(text+voice+fmt)}"`
    2. If key exists, return cached bytes
    3. Else call `provider.synthesize(...)`, store in Redis with `SETEX(key, CACHE_TTL, data)`, return bytes.
• Make TTL configurable (default 30 days).
subtasks.testStrategy: Pytest with local Redis container: first call should miss, second call should hit. Assert identical byte sequence.

subtasks.title: Expose POST /api/tts Endpoint
subtasks.description: Add FastAPI route that accepts JSON `{text, voice?, format?}` and streams audio back as `audio/mpeg` or `audio/ogg` with proper headers.
subtasks.details:
• Create router `tts_router` in `backend/api/tts.py`.
• Request model: `TTSRequest(text: constr(min_length=1, max_length=1000), voice:str='Joanna', format:Literal['mp3','ogg']='mp3')`.
• Use `get_or_generate_audio` to fetch bytes.
• Return `StreamingResponse` with `media_type` mapped from format.
• Add error handling: 400 for empty text, 413 for >1000 chars.
• Register router in main `app` under `/api` prefix.
subtasks.testStrategy: HTTP call using `starlette.testclient`: POST sample text, assert 200, correct `content-type`, and `len(content) > 1000`.

subtasks.title: Comprehensive Unit & Integration Tests
subtasks.description: Write automated tests covering provider selection, caching logic, and endpoint behavior with mocked external dependencies.
subtasks.details:
• Use `pytest` + `pytest-asyncio`.
• Mock AWS Polly with `botocore.stub.Stubber`, mock Coqui by monkeypatching `synthesize` to return dummy bytes.
• Spin up ephemeral Redis via `pytest-redis` fixture.
• Endpoint tests: verify cache hit path by inspecting Redis calls.
• Edge-case tests: empty text, unsupported format, >1000 chars, Redis down (should still synthesize and return 200).
subtasks.testStrategy: CI executes `pytest -q`; coverage report ≥90% for `backend/tts` package.

subtasks.title: Docker & CI Integration for TTS Service
subtasks.description: Update Dockerfiles, docker-compose, and GitHub Actions to include TTS dependencies, Redis service, and provider credentials/models.
subtasks.details:
• Backend Dockerfile: install `boto3`, `redis`, `tts`, download Coqui model via `RUN python -m tts --list_models | head -1 | xargs python -m tts --download`.
• docker-compose: add `redis` service (alpine image) and mount `TTS_MODELS_DIR` volume.
• Add build args/envs for AWS keys (passed via secrets in GitHub Actions).
• Update CI workflow to run Redis, execute tests, and cache Coqui models between runs using actions/cache.
• Document env variables and sample curl command in `README`.
subtasks.testStrategy: CI step: `docker compose up -d`, wait, then `curl -X POST localhost:8000/api/tts -d '{"text":"ping"}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --output ping.mp3 && test -s ping.mp3`.

tasks.title: Frontend Conversation UI
tasks.description: Single-page web app to display conversation, control mic, and play TTS audio.
tasks.details:
• React components: WakeIndicator, TranscriptBubble, AnswerBubble, LatencyChip
• WebSocket to backend for streaming
• Auto-scroll log; show spinner while waiting (<3s SLA)
• Add disclosure when confidence<0.75
tasks.testStrategy: Storybook visual tests; Cypress end-to-end – click mic, speak sample, expect bubbles and audio playback.

subtasks.title: Bootstrap Conversation SPA and Core Components
subtasks.description: Create the single-page React application shell and stub out all visual components required for the conversation UI.
subtasks.details:
• Generate a Vite + React + TypeScript project inside /frontend if not already present.
• Add React Router with single route “/” rendering <ConversationPage/>.
• Inside ConversationPage scaffold Contexts: ConversationContext (holds messages, pending state) and UiConfigContext (theme, TTS on/off).
• Implement placeholder functional components (exported individually and in Storybook): WakeIndicator, TranscriptBubble, AnswerBubble, LatencyChip, MicButton. Include minimal props and CSS modules / styled-components.
• Wire Storybook stories for each component and the page container to enable visual regression tests.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run Storybook, capture reference screenshots for each component using Chromatic or Percy. Jest snapshot test for component render without crashing.

subtasks.title: Implement WebSocket Streaming Client & Context Provider
subtasks.description: Create a reusable hook and provider that manages the WebSocket connection to the backend and normalises incoming/outgoing message payloads.
subtasks.details:
• Add useWebSocket hook (leveraging native WebSocket API or ‘reconnecting-websocket’ npm pkg).
• Protocol: {type: "transcript"|"answer", text, confidence?, audioUrl?, latencyMs?}.
• Expose sendTranscript(text:string) and connectionState via WebSocketContext.
• Handle incremental backend messages (streaming chunks) by aggregating into final answer.
• Provide mock server script (ws npm pkg) for local development that echos messages with artificial latency to allow offline testing.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test hook with jest + mock-ws verifying reconnect logic and message aggregation. Cypress test hitting mock server ensuring connection opens, message sent, response received.

subtasks.title: Conversation Log Rendering, Auto-Scroll & Latency Feedback
subtasks.description: Render the scrolling list of TranscriptBubble and AnswerBubble components, show spinner while awaiting answer (<3 s SLA), and display LatencyChip once response received.
subtasks.details:
• Create <ConversationLog/> component that consumes ConversationContext and maps messages to bubbles.
• Implement auto-scroll (scrollIntoView on last element within useEffect).
• When sendTranscript is called, push a ‘pending’ AnswerBubble with spinner; start timer; if backend answer not received within 3 s, visually emphasise spinner (red border) to highlight SLA breach.
• Once answer arrives, replace spinner with final AnswerBubble and append <LatencyChip latencyMs={msg.latencyMs}/>.
• Ensure virtualised list (react-window) if >100 messages to keep perf.
subtasks.testStrategy: Jest DOM tests: send synthetic messages, assert auto-scroll by checking scrollTop. Storybook interaction test: simulate 2 s vs 4 s latency and verify spinner styling. Cypress e2e with mock server returning after 1 s.

subtasks.title: Mic Control, WakeIndicator Integration & TTS Audio Playback
subtasks.description: Wire microphone button to wake-word service (Task 4) and STT pipeline (Task 5), forward final transcript to backend, and play returned TTS audio streams.
subtasks.details:
• Consume WakeWordContext (provided by Task 4) to show WakeIndicator active state.
• Implement MicButton behaviour: when wake detected or user clicks, start STT capture via SpeechToTextContext (Task 5). Stop capture on silence and call sendTranscript.
• Upon receiving answer message with audioUrl, create Audio object or use Web Audio API, preload, then play; maintain queue if multiple audios overlap.
• Persist mute/volume preference in localStorage.
• Handle browser autoplay policies by requesting user gesture on first play.
subtasks.testStrategy: Cypress end-to-end: load page, simulate wake word event, inject prerecorded audio, assert TranscriptBubble appears, verify audio element plays (spy on HTMLMediaElement.play). Unit test audio queue logic with jest-fake-timers.

subtasks.title: Confidence Disclosure & Accessibility Pass
subtasks.description: Display an explicit disclaimer when backend answer confidence < 0.75 and ensure UI meets accessibility guidelines.
subtasks.details:
• Extend AnswerBubble props with confidence?:number. If value <0.75, append small italic text: “(May be inaccurate)” and aria-label conveying same.
• Add tooltip with explanation triggered on hover or focus.
• In ConversationLog, announce low-confidence answers via ARIA live region for screen readers.
• Run axe-core accessibility scan and fix any violations (contrast, keyboard navigation).
subtasks.testStrategy: Jest test: render AnswerBubble with 0.6 confidence, assert disclaimer text exists. Cypress a11y plugin to scan page. Unit test ARIA live region receives update.

tasks.title: Latency & Metrics Collection
tasks.description: Measure time from STT final to TTS stream start and push to Prometheus.
tasks.details:
• Backend middleware timestamps query receipt and TTS start
• Expose /metrics for Prometheus with histogram bucket ≤3s
• Frontend send client timestamps for total conversational latency
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: mock 100 queries, assert 80th percentile <3s. Grafana dashboard visualization.

subtasks.title: Implement backend latency timestamping middleware
subtasks.description: Capture precise timestamps for the moment an STT-final transcript is received and the moment the TTS audio-stream starts, and store the delta in request context.
subtasks.details:
• In the API service (e.g. Node/Express or Python/FastAPI), add a middleware placed immediately after authentication.
• On POST /api/query (or equivalent) set ctx.sttReceived = process.hrtime.bigint() (nanoseconds).
• Extend the TTS start routine (first chunk write or websocket ‘start’) to call ctx.ttsStart = process.hrtime.bigint() and compute latencyMs = Number(ctx.ttsStart - ctx.sttReceived) / 1_000_000.
• Attach latencyMs to the request object or emit an internal event “latencyRecorded” {latencyMs, conversationId} so later code can push it to Prometheus.
• Make sure the middleware does not block the response path; use async hooks / CLS for context in concurrent environments.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test with jest/pytest: mock request cycle, assert both timestamps exist and delta >0.

subtasks.title: Add Prometheus histogram and record backend latency
subtasks.description: Integrate Prometheus client library and push the latency metric captured in 8.1 into a histogram with ≤3 s upper bucket.
subtasks.details:
• Install prom-client (Node) or prometheus-client (Python).
• Define const sttToTtsLatency = new Histogram({ name: 'stt_to_tts_latency_seconds', help: 'Latency from STT final to TTS start', buckets: [0.1,0.25,0.5,1,2,3] }); // last bucket ≤3 s.
• In the handler that receives latencyMs from 8.1, call sttToTtsLatency.observe(latencyMs/1000).
• Register the histogram with the default registry.
• Ensure high-cardinality labels are avoided; include optional label stage="prod|staging".
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: feed known latencies, scrape metrics endpoint locally, assert _bucket counts increment correctly.

subtasks.title: Expose /metrics HTTP endpoint for Prometheus scraping
subtasks.description: Create a publicly reachable (but authenticated or IP-restricted) /metrics route that dumps Prometheus metrics, including the histogram from 8.2.
subtasks.details:
• For Express: app.get('/metrics', async (req,res)=>{ res.set('Content-Type', register.contentType); res.send(await register.metrics()); });
• For FastAPI: @app.get('/metrics') def metrics(): return PlainTextResponse(generate_latest(REGISTRY)).
• Add optional basic-auth or network ACL.
• Update Kubernetes/Compose manifest: add ServiceMonitor or scrape_configs for job_name: ‘tts-service’ with 15 s interval.
subtasks.testStrategy: Curl /metrics locally; expect HTTP 200 and presence of stt_to_tts_latency_seconds_bucket lines.

subtasks.title: Collect total conversational latency from frontend
subtasks.description: Instrument the browser app to timestamp wake-word detection, STT final, and TTS playback start, then POST aggregated latency to the backend for Prometheus ingestion.
subtasks.details:
• In React context, on wake-word detection set window.perf.wakeTs = performance.now().
• On STT final transcript, set perf.sttFinalTs.
• When audio element starts playing TTS stream, set perf.ttsPlayTs and compute totalLatency = ttsPlayTs - wakeTs.
• POST JSON {totalLatencyMs, conversationId} to /api/latency.
• Backend endpoint validates payload and stt_total_latency_seconds.observe(totalLatencyMs/1000) using another histogram with buckets [0.5,1,2,3,5].
subtasks.testStrategy: Cypress test: stub Date.now() to deterministic values, simulate events, assert POST body and backend metric increment.

subtasks.title: Integration load test & Grafana dashboard
subtasks.description: Automate 100-query load test, verify 80th percentile <3 s, and create Grafana panel visualizing both backend and total latencies.
subtasks.details:
• Write k6 or Locust script that calls /api/query, waits for websocket frame ‘tts_start’, records latency; run 100 iterations.
• After run, scrape Prometheus /api/v1/query_quantile(0.8, stt_to_tts_latency_seconds) and assert <3.
• Provision Grafana dashboard JSON with 2 histogram panels + 1 percentile graph; commit to repo under grafana/latency_dashboard.json.
• Optional alert rule: if 0.9 quantile >3 s for 5 m, fire ‘High Latency’.
subtasks.testStrategy: CI job executes k6 script; fails pipeline if assertion violated. Manually open Grafana URL to confirm panels render.

tasks.title: Confidence & Clarification Logic
tasks.description: Implement fallback ‘I don’t know’ or ask clarifying question when confidence low.
tasks.details:
• Extend AnswerGenerator: if 0.4<conf<0.75 => ask follow-up based on missing slot; <0.4 => plain I don’t know
• Maintain session context to handle follow-up replies.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: feed ambiguous queries and assert follow-up question generated. Acceptance: manual dialogue.

subtasks.title: Refactor AnswerGenerator to Support Response Types & Confidence Flags
subtasks.description: Extend the existing AnswerGenerator interface to return a structured object that contains the generated answer text, numeric confidence score, and a new enum `response_type` with values [`ANSWER`, `CLARIFY`, `FALLBACK`].  All downstream logic (API layer, Web-socket dispatcher, etc.) must be updated to consume the new shape without breaking current behaviour.
subtasks.details:
• Create `ResponseType` enum in `answer_generator/types.py`.
• Update `Answer` dataclass to include `response_type: ResponseType`.
• Modify all internal call sites (controller, web handlers, unit tests) to use the new field.
• Keep default `response_type=ANSWER` when not explicitly set so existing tests pass.
• Add migration notes in CHANGELOG.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit: assert that a high-confidence query returns `response_type==ANSWER`. Contract test: JSON schema validation for API `/api/query` response.

subtasks.title: Implement Low-Confidence Fallback (<0.4) Logic
subtasks.description: Add logic that classifies any response whose confidence score is <0.40 as `FALLBACK` and generates the canonical "I’m not sure about that" answer text.
subtasks.details:
• In `answer_generator/strategies.py`, after computing confidence, branch: if `conf < 0.4` ➜ set `response_type=FALLBACK`, `text="I’m not sure about that."`.
• Make threshold value `CONF_FALLBACK_THRESHOLD` configurable via env var with default 0.4.
• Ensure no DB look-ups are executed after selecting fallback to save latency.
subtasks.testStrategy: Pytest parameterised: pass synthetic queries with forced low confidence scores, assert `response_type==FALLBACK` and text matches exactly.

subtasks.title: Generate Clarifying Questions for Medium Confidence (0.4–0.75)
subtasks.description: When confidence is between 0.40 and 0.75, identify missing slots in the parsed query and craft an explicit follow-up question to clarify the user’s intent, marking the response as `CLARIFY`.
subtasks.details:
• Extend parser output to include `required_slots` vs `filled_slots`.
• Implement `clarification_builder.build(slot_name)` that maps each slot to a human-readable question (e.g., slot `flight_no` ➜ "Which flight number are you asking about?").
• In AnswerGenerator, if `0.4 <= conf < 0.75` and there is at least one missing required slot, pick highest-priority missing slot and call builder.
• Set `response_type=CLARIFY` and store `pending_slot` metadata in the Answer payload.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit: feed a query with ambiguous flight reference, assert returned question references the missing slot and `response_type==CLARIFY`.

subtasks.title: Add Session Context Manager for Follow-Up Handling
subtasks.description: Maintain conversational state so that answers to clarifying questions are merged with the original query on the next turn.  Persist pending slots, original utterance, and timestamp inside a short-lived session object (Redis or in-memory for MVP).
subtasks.details:
• Implement `SessionContext` class with fields: `session_id`, `original_query`, `pending_slots`, `expires_at`.
• Wire context storage into FastAPI dependency that injects the manager on each request.
• On receiving a user reply, check if session has `pending_slots`; if so, merge new slot values and rerun AnswerGenerator in `ANSWER` mode.
• Auto-expire sessions after 5 min or when all slots filled.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test using Starlette TestClient: simulate 3-turn conversation (ambiguous ➜ clarify ➜ final answer) and assert correct progression of response types.

subtasks.title: End-to-End Dialogue Tests & Threshold Tuning
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive test suite and scripts that validate the complete confidence & clarification flow, and fine-tune threshold values based on empirical data.
subtasks.details:
• Build dialogue test harness that plays predefined utterance sequences and captures API responses.
• Include cases: high-confidence direct answer, low-confidence fallback, medium-confidence clarification followed by successful answer, and session timeout.
• Record latency and accuracy metrics; adjust `CONF_FALLBACK_THRESHOLD` & `CONF_CLARIFY_THRESHOLD` (default 0.75) if needed.
• Document results in `/docs/confidence_tuning.md`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run test harness in CI; fail build if any dialogue path deviates from expected `response_type` sequence or message content.

tasks.title: End-to-End Demo & Deployment
tasks.description: Dockerized deployment to AWS Fargate + CloudFront for frontend, plus demo recording scripts.
tasks.details:
• `docker compose -f prod.yml` build
• CD pipeline pushes image to ECR, Terraform ECS service, S3 + CloudFront
• Script `scripts/record_demo.sh` using ffmpeg to capture browser & mic
• Write <1page technical summary and hours spent
tasks.testStrategy: Smoke test on staging URL: open site, say test Q, hear correct answer. Record 3-minute video and verify playback.

subtasks.title: Production Docker Build & Local Validation
subtasks.description: Create optimized multi-stage Dockerfiles for backend and frontend, add prod-ready docker-compose file, and validate the stack locally.
subtasks.details:
• Refactor existing Dockerfiles into multi-stage builds (builder → runtime) with non-root user and healthcheck.
• Add docker-compose.prod.yml referencing the built images, env vars, secrets, and a SQLite volume.
• Enable BuildKit and .dockerignore to minimize context.
• Provide make targets: `make build-prod` and `make run-prod`.
• Update README with one-liner for local smoke test.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d`; hit http://localhost:8000/health and / (frontend) expecting HTTP 200 within 5 s.

subtasks.title: CI/CD Pipeline: Build & Push Image to ECR
subtasks.description: Automate building the production image and publishing it to AWS Elastic Container Registry on every push to main.
subtasks.details:
• Create `.github/workflows/cd.yml` with jobs: checkout → set-up QEMU/buildx → login to ECR via AWS OIDC → build & push image tagged with `${{ github.sha }}` and `latest`.
• Store AWS account, region, and repo name as GitHub secrets.
• Cache Docker layers for faster builds.
• Post-build step: upload image digest as workflow artifact for Terraform.
• Document manual trigger using workflow_dispatch.
subtasks.testStrategy: Push a dummy commit; verify workflow passes and image appears in ECR with both tags; pull image locally and run `docker run` healthcheck.

subtasks.title: Terraform AWS Infrastructure (ECS Fargate + S3/CloudFront)
subtasks.description: Provision all cloud resources required for the demo using Terraform and hook them into the CD pipeline.
subtasks.details:
• Under /infra, add Terraform modules: networking (VPC, subnets), IAM roles, ECS cluster + task definition referencing `${aws_ecr_repository.app.repository_url}:latest`, Application Load Balancer, and ECS Service (Fargate 0.25 vCPU / 0.5 GB).
• Create S3 bucket for frontend build artifacts and enable static hosting; generate CloudFront distribution with OAI, default root object index.html, TLS via ACM (us-east-1).
• Configure CloudFront invalidation GitHub Action step post-deploy.
• Store remote state in S3 with dynamo DB lock.
• Provide `make deploy` wrapper executing `terraform init/plan/apply`.
• Output variables: api_url, frontend_url, cloudfront_domain.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `terraform apply` in a temp AWS account; check ECS service health in console; curl `${cloudfront_domain}` returns index.html; open `${api_url}/health` returns 200.

subtasks.title: Demo Recording Script with FFmpeg
subtasks.description: Create cross-platform bash script `scripts/record_demo.sh` to record browser window, microphone, and cursor, saving an MP4 ready for publication.
subtasks.details:
• For Linux: use `xvfb-run google-chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222 <URL>` then `ffmpeg -f x11grab -video_size 1920x1080 -i $DISPLAY -f pulse -i default -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -c:a aac -b:a 128k demos/demo_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).mp4`.
• For macOS: detect OS; fall back to `ffmpeg -f avfoundation -i "<screen index>:<mic index>" ...`.
• Accept CLI args: `--url`, `--duration`, `--outfile`.
• Add README section and install instructions (`brew install ffmpeg` / `apt-get install ffmpeg`).
• Provide Make target `make record-demo` that opens CloudFront URL and records 180 s.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run script for 10 s; verify output file exists, plays video + audio, and size >0 MB. Use `ffprobe` to assert video & audio streams present.

subtasks.title: Smoke Test, Demo Capture & Technical Summary
subtasks.description: Execute end-to-end smoke test on the staging URL, record demo video, and write <1-page technical summary including time spent.
subtasks.details:
• Open `${frontend_url}` from Terraform output; ask sample question (e.g., "What gate is flight UA12 at?") and confirm correct TTS response.
• If pass, run `make record-demo --duration 180` to capture full walkthrough; commit resulting MP4 under /docs/demos or upload to S3 public bucket and link.
• Draft `docs/technical_summary.md` (<300 words) covering architecture, deployment flow, and total hours logged per subtask; include link to video.
• Add both artifacts to PR and tag stakeholders.
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Manual: Follow smoke-test script, ensure bubbles render and audio plays within 3 s.
2. Confirm summary length ≤1 page via `wc -w`.
3. Reviewer downloads MP4 and watches first 30 s without glitches.
